tldr: C33 (upload-duplicate-status) is done: backend/app/api/v1/documents.py now returns HTTP 200 (instead of 201) for an idempotent duplicate upload of an already-ready document via response.status_code = status.HTTP_200_OK on that branch; new uploads remain 201. test_duplicate_upload_is_idempotent updated to assert 200. Claude-direct (Eran-approved), 16/16 tests pass. C34 (database-test-container-command, Adam) is next.

open_handoffs.regarding: C38-C49 — use EmbeddingService.embed_documents()/embed_query() for corpus/query vectors; LLMService.chat() only for generation; profile on EmbeddingService.profile
open_handoffs.detail: EmbeddingService and LLMService contracts are frozen by C25. Policy retrieval and generation commits must import only from backend/app/services/llm.py and never use provider SDK types directly. C27 ingestion already uses EmbeddingService.embed_documents() exclusively.

open_handoffs.regarding: C55-C56 — catch only LLMError and subclasses in route handlers, never provider SDK exceptions
open_handoffs.detail: All SDK types are isolated in backend/app/services/llm.py. Route handlers must import LLMError hierarchy only.

open_handoffs.regarding: C28 — create/resolve the policy_documents row and call the frozen ingest_document() contract
open_handoffs.detail: backend/app/services/ingestion.py exposes async ingest_document(*, document_id, file_bytes, filename, content_type, db, embeddings) -> IngestResult. The route handler creates the policy_documents row (status='processing') and calls this; the service owns the processing -> ready|failed transition and never raises to the caller.

open_handoffs.regarding: Future frontend document work — safe document metadata shape from GET /api/v1/documents and GET /api/v1/documents/{id}
open_handoffs.detail: Both endpoints return id, title, original_filename, status, chunk_count, uploaded_by, embedding_provider/embedding_model/embedding_dimensions, uploaded_at, updated_at, and failure_code (one of DocumentFailureCode, only set when status == 'failed'). Never chunk text, embeddings, or file_path.

open_handoffs.regarding: C39-C49 — retrieval and citations may assume deterministic chunk indexes and page/section metadata produced by chunk_blocks()
open_handoffs.detail: chunk_blocks() in backend/app/services/ingestion.py produces sequential chunk_index values and preserves page_number/section provenance per chunk, deterministically across re-ingestion. Policy retrieval and citation logic can rely on this for stable references.

replan_history.trigger: Phase 1 complete, Phase 2 scope now defined
replan_history.summary: Added 10 commits (C23-C32) implementing manifesto-spec.md §Phase 2 'Policy RAG': pgvector storage (C23), LLMService implementation (C24), document ingestion (C25), document upload routes (C26), RAG policy pipeline (C27), policy chat routes (C28), conversation persistence (C29), policy chat UI (C30), conversation sidebar UI (C31), citations UI (C32). No commits removed or reordered — pure addition after C22.

replan_history.trigger: Eran requested a production RAG and software-design review of the pending Phase 2 commits
replan_history.summary: Corrected the dependency graph and stale file assumptions; expanded and cleanly renumbered the pending sequence as C24-C34 with no letter suffixes. Revised the plan around a deployment-wide embedding profile, idempotent structured ingestion, HNSW plus lexical hybrid retrieval, grounded source labels, versioned SSE, cancellation, durable message/citation states, cursor pagination, and frontend race/error handling. Added a Phase 3 graph-aware logistics RAG decision.

replan_history.trigger: Eran requested a second decomposition pass using the approved task-to-commit guides and C29 limits
replan_history.summary: Replaced the preliminary C30-C43 roadmap with bounded behavior candidates, then finalized the pending range as C30-C76 after permanent hook ownership made the planned ownership commit redundant. Each pending row represents one owner and one observable behavior across workflow telemetry, recovery, policy RAG, backend chat, frontend chat, and assembled verification.

open_issues.title: No automated Phase 1 backend test coverage
open_issues.detail: pytest infrastructure exists (C24) but only covers config.py. All Phase 1 routes (auth, admin, inventory), the JWT helpers, and the ORM model constraints have zero automated test coverage. Any regression in Phase 1 code during Phase 2 work is undetectable. Prioritize before C28 (policy chat routes) which will build on the Phase 1 auth and admin layers.

open_issues.title: Rollback protocol untested
open_issues.detail: rollback_history is empty. The rollback path (alembic downgrade, container teardown, re-migration) has never been exercised against the actual schema. When C26 runs a schema migration for HNSW, the ability to safely roll back is unverified. Test the downgrade path before any irreversible schema migration lands.

open_issues.title: Orchestrator post-session token costs untracked
open_issues.detail: TOKEN_RECORDS.md tracks per-invocation token counts for implementor and reviewer agents. Claude's own orchestration tokens (boot, verification passes, correction rounds, commit writing) are never measured or included in session totals. True cost per commit is understated. Add an 'orchestrator' row to TOKEN_RECORDS schema and instrument it. Feasibility note (2026-06-13): Claude Code's session transcript (transcript_path, available to hooks) carries a `usage` block per assistant turn, including the orchestrator's own (input_tokens + output_tokens + cache_creation_input_tokens + cache_read_input_tokens). A Stop-hook could sum these across the orchestrator's turns for a commit, excluding Agent/Task subagent turns (already captured via tool_cap). Main tradeoff: the transcript format is not a stable public API, so this would be somewhat brittle and requires a new hook plus schema fields in TOKEN_RECORDS.md and CONTEXT_METRICS.json. Follow-up requested by Eran (2026-06-13), three dashboard gaps for Claude-direct commits: (1) Tokens — show the orchestrator's actual token usage for the commit (per the feasibility note above), not 'Not tracked'. (2) Selected/used files column — for delegated commits this shows package selected_files vs files actually read; Claude-direct has no package, but should show an equivalent 'files read vs files in Files To Modify Or Add' count so the same column is populated. (3) Orchestrator tool calls — context_telemetry.py --start-orchestrator/--stop-orchestrator (built in C30/C31) already records a per-commit tool_calls count but is currently never invoked for Claude-direct commits (C30-C33 all show 'unavailable'/'Not tracked'); wiring this in for Claude-direct commits would give Eran visibility into exactly how many tool calls were used, with no new hook needed.

open_issues.title: No cross-commit regression test gate
open_issues.detail: Each commit's test gate covers only the tests added or explicitly run for that commit. There is no protocol step that runs the full test suite before approving a commit. A Phase 2 agent could break a Phase 1 test and the regression would go undetected until someone runs pytest manually. Add a 'run full suite' gate before every approval in the commit loop, once coverage is sufficient to make it meaningful.

open_issues.title: Quinn activation criterion must be decided before C27
open_issues.detail: Resolved 2026-06-10 (D32): C27 meets the activation criterion. Quinn runs a coverage review on C27 after Nova's implementation, alongside the standard gate-triage.

open_issues.title: prepare_agent_delegation.py cache freshness unverified for C25
open_issues.detail: The Context Package V2 cache (D29) was introduced as shadow-mode only. Before C25 is invoked, the codebase graph cache should be validated against the current repo state: run prepare_agent_delegation.py with --commit 25 --agent nova and inspect the generated brief for stale paths, missing contracts, or wrong dependency edges. Do not invoke Nova without confirming the brief is accurate.

open_issues.title: verify-commit is structural only — perception gap vs. quality gate
open_issues.detail: CLAUDE.md Step 7b and team-preferences.md both describe '/verify-commit' as a required quality gate. verify_constraints.py checks three things: context block present, no forbidden-path files, tool budget respected. It does NOT check: test results, logic correctness, doc currency, handoff completeness, or whether changes implement the spec. The name and placement in the protocol imply a quality guarantee the tool cannot provide. Either rename it to 'structural-check' and update all references, or document its scope limitations prominently everywhere it is referenced.

open_issues.title: Host port 5432 conflict blocks DB-touching test/migration runs from the host
open_issues.detail: A native Windows PostgreSQL 18 service (postgresql-x64-18) listens on 0.0.0.0:5432 and intercepts connections meant for the dockerized manifesto-db-1 (also mapped to 5432), so host-side asyncpg/alembic/pytest get InvalidPasswordError against the wrong server. Same issue previously hit C07 (worked around by running migrations inside the Docker container). C26's alembic upgrade/downgrade and pytest run for test_policy_storage.py were skipped for this reason (Eran's call). Fix options: (1) stop/disable postgresql-x64-18, (2) always run DB-touching commands via `docker compose run`/`exec` against the backend container (C07 pattern) — document this as the standard in backend.md, or (3) remap the docker-compose db port. Any future commit with DB-dependent tests (C27 ingestion, C29 RAG pipeline, C31 conversation persistence) will hit this again until resolved.

open_issues.title: Verify PyMuPDF memory behavior on a pathological/oversized PDF
open_issues.detail: Sage flagged a CRITICAL on C27 (PyMuPDF decompression-bomb risk during PDF page extraction). Dismissed per D33 as a likely false positive — `_MAX_PAGES` is checked before per-page extraction begins, and the spec frames upload size limits (C28) as a layered defense. C28 (D36) reaffirmed: open. MAX_DOCUMENT_UPLOAD_BYTES (25 MiB) is one layer but does not resolve in-memory decompression risk during page extraction. Needs a live test with a pathological/oversized PDF to confirm bounded memory growth before the page-count check triggers.

open_issues.title: Document upload failure_code mapping is coupled to ingestion.py's exact error message strings
open_issues.detail: C28's _failure_code_for() in backend/app/api/v1/documents.py maps PolicyDocument.error_message text to a stable DocumentFailureCode via _FAILURE_CODE_BY_MESSAGE, a hardcoded dict of ingestion.py's sanitized error strings (see app.services.ingestion._sanitize_error and the IngestionError subclasses). Any change to those message strings silently breaks the mapping (falls back to 'internal_error'). Sage flagged HIGH but non-blocking (D36). Recommend Nova add a structured error code to IngestResult/policy_documents and replace the string mapping with a direct lookup.

open_issues.title: tests/models/test_policy_storage.py (C26) hardcodes localhost:5432 DB_URL — 7 errors when run via docker compose
open_issues.detail: Same root cause as the fix applied in C28's tests/api/test_documents.py: DB_URL = "postgresql+asyncpg://manifesto:manifesto@localhost:5432/manifesto" resolves to the wrong host inside the backend container (should read DATABASE_URL env var, which resolves to db:5432). Currently produces 7 errors when the full suite is run via `docker compose run --rm backend uv run pytest`. Out of scope for C28; apply the same os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL", ...) fix in a future commit.

open_issues.title: hooks/tool_cap.json undercounts implementor tokens vs. the Agent tool's <usage> block
open_issues.detail: Resolved 2026-06-11: extract_tokens() in hooks/tool_cap_end.py summed only input_tokens + output_tokens from the usage object, dropping cache_creation_input_tokens and cache_read_input_tokens. For C29B's repair invocation, the usage object {input:2, cache_creation:1038, cache_read:48008, output:789} sums to exactly 49,837 (the harness's reported subagent_tokens), but the old formula returned 791. Fixed extract_tokens() to sum all four fields; added regression tests using this exact usage object (test_extract_tokens_includes_cache_tokens, test_close_invocation_records_full_usage_total). ORCHESTRATION.md now documents that token budgets are post-hoc, cross-invocation circuit breakers (PostToolUse on the Agent matcher fires once, after the subagent has already finished, so a hard stop blocks only the next invocation) and that max_total_tokens covers only Agent-tool usage, not orchestrator usage. hooks/tool_cap.json's stale C29B record (791/3105) was left unchanged per Eran's instruction — TOKEN_RECORDS.md and CONTEXT_METRICS.json already carry the corrected 49,837.

notes: The approved task-to-commit decomposition defines C30-C76. C29A (preflight-score-engine) builds and persists deterministic preflight scoring and hard blockers as a standalone script. C29B (preflight-delegation-gate) wires that script into prepare_agent_delegation.py as a hard pre-delegation gate. C29C (preflight-dashboard-details) visualizes each persisted report in the dashboard. C30 and C33 depend on C29C, so every later commit inherits all three prerequisites transitively; runtime delegation also enforces preflight on every commit from C29C onward. C29A is the only full bootstrap exception per commit-protocol.md rule 17 (revised): before invoking C29B, Claude manually runs `python hooks/preflight_commit.py --commit 29B --agent adam --json` and requires score >= 80 with zero blocking_violations; automatic enforcement inside prepare_agent_delegation.py begins after C29B, for C29C onward. C29A's first invocation (2026-06-11) returned SPLIT_REQUIRED after exhausting its 18-call budget on discovery with zero files written; the spec was rewritten to a narrower preflight-score-engine scope and the original combined scope was split into C29A/C29B/C29C (was C29A/C29B). C29A's second invocation (2026-06-11, greenfield budget) completed: hooks/preflight_commit.py (647 lines) + hooks/tests/test_preflight_commit.py (431 lines, 13 tests) committed as 69b3658; verify_constraints all_pass; full hooks suite 120 passed/1 skipped. Adam's token usage for this invocation was lost to context compaction (recorded as '— (lost)' in TOKEN_RECORDS.md).
historical_notes: C23 (`pgvector-migration`, Rex) resolved 2026-06-08 with NO git commit — investigation (Rex, on Eran's instruction to invoke him despite the scope already looking thin) found the entire pgvector/policy schema (extension, both tables, FKs, ivfflat index) already present in `0001_initial.py` lines 101-141, dated 2026-06-05 alongside the C06 model commit (`f712343`). Verified live: `alembic current` = 0001_initial (head), pgvector v0.8.2 active, tables/FKs/index/ORM mapping all correct. Writing a new migration would have duplicated DDL or falsified history; downgrading would have torn down the entire schema. Full record in DECISIONS.md D28. commit-protocol.md C23 marked done-by-prior-work; pointer advanced straight to C24 (Nova). Nova handoff re: VECTOR(1536) vs Ollama 768-dim recorded in open_handoffs above — must be resolved before C25 ingestion begins. || C22 committed 2026-06-07 (47bca18) — loginApi fixed to send JSON {email, password}, verified clean (tsc + network-level contract check by Claude). Eran then manually click-through-verified all 4 remaining browser checks: (1) /login form renders correctly, (2) admin login (admin@manifesto.local/admin123) redirects to /dashboard, (3) /dashboard shows 'Coming soon' placeholder, (4) manager login (manager.smoke@manifesto.local/manager123) navigating to /admin redirects away — to /login specifically. Item 4 initially looked surprising (redirecting an authenticated user to the login page), but Claude confirmed via ProtectedRoute.tsx (lines 12-18) that both the no-token AND wrong-role paths intentionally redirect to /login — documented by Aria in her C18 worklog. Logged as D25 (UX trade-off noted for possible Phase 2+ refinement, not a blocker). All C21 SMOKE_TEST_RESULTS.md items now resolved — Phase 1 formally closed 2026-06-07. Adam's throwaway manager user (manager.smoke@manifesto.local/manager123) remains in the dev DB as a useful fixture for future role-based testing. Phase 2 replan applied same day: C23-C32 added per manifesto-spec.md §Phase 2 (Policy RAG). Nova activated — identity file at .claude/agents/ai-engineer.md, first commit is C24. Sequence: C23(Rex) → C24(Nova) → {C25(Nova), C26(Rex)} → C27(Nova) → C28(Rex) → {C29(Rex), C30(Aria)} → C31(Aria) → C32(Aria). Viktor's next batch wave lands at C25 (every-5th-commit cadence continues from C20).
